questions.q: For a perfectly competitive firm, the supply curve is the same as the marginal cost curve. What is the shape of the supply curve for a perfectly competitive firm?
What is the meaning of the term 'marginal cost'?
questions.answer: The additional cost of producing one more unit of a commodity.

questions.q: What is meant by the 'law of increasing marginal cost'?
questions.answer: As the level of production increases, the marginal cost of production also increases.

questions.q: In a perfectly competitive market, the supply curve of a firm is downward sloping. What is the reason for this?
questions.answer: The supply curve of a firm in a perfectly competitive market is downward sloping because the firm can increase its profit by producing more units of the commodity as long as price is greater than the marginal cost.

questions.q: The supply curve of a firm is U-shaped. What does this indicate?
questions.answer: The supply curve of a firm is U-shaped because the marginal cost curve of a firm is U-shaped.

questions.q: What is the condition for a firm to supply more units of a commodity in the short run?
questions.answer: The condition for a firm to supply more units of a commodity in the short run is that the price of the commodity is greater than the marginal cost.

questions.q: The short-run supply curve of a firm is upward sloping. Does the firm operate in the short run? Justify your answer.
questions.answer: Yes, the firm operates in the short run because the short-run supply curve of a firm is upward sloping.

questions.q: What does the marginal cost curve of a firm represent?
questions.answer: The marginal cost curve of a firm represents the additional cost of producing one more unit of a commodity.

questions.q: In a perfectly competitive market, the supply curve of a firm is a horizontal line. What does this indicate?
questions.answer: The supply curve of a firm in a perfectly competitive market is a horizontal line because the firm can sell any quantity at the given price.

questions.q: A firm is maximizing its profit when the price is equal to the marginal cost. Is this statement correct? Justify your answer.
questions.answer: No, this statement is incorrect because a firm maximizes its profit when the price is greater than the marginal cost.
